data.title: NorthWestern Q1 revenue slightly misses estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. utility's Q1 revenue slightly missed analyst expectations, adjusted EPS beat consensus

  • GAAP EPS fell yr/yr due to higher costs, while adjusted EPS rose

  • Company affirms 2026 earnings guidance and reports progress on Black Hills merger

Outlook

  • NorthWestern affirms 2026 non-GAAP EPS guidance of $3.68 to $3.83 per diluted share

  • Company maintains long-term EPS and rate base growth guidance of 4% to 6%

  • NorthWestern expects to close merger with Black Hills in second half of 2026

Result Drivers

  • HIGHER OPERATING COSTS - Increased operating, administrative, and merger-related costs, as well as expenses tied to new Colstrip ownership, weighed on results

  • NEW RATES AND TRANSMISSION REVENUE - Higher base rates and increased transmission revenues partially offset cost pressures

  • LOWER RETAIL VOLUMES - Declines in electric and natural gas retail volumes due to unfavorable weather impacted revenue

Company press release:

Analyst Coverage

  • The average consensus recommendation for the electric utilities peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for NorthWestern Energy Group Inc is $70.00, about 1.9% below its April 29 closing price of $71.34

  • The stock recently traded at 19 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 17 three months ago

data.title: Markel Group Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Markel Group Inc reported a quarterly adjusted loss of $41.69​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $21.60. The mean expectation of five analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $26.34 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $25.17 to $27.41 per share.

  • Revenue fell 16.9% to $2.82 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $3.64 billion.

  • Markel Group Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of $18.90​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $212.29 million.

  • Markel Group Inc shares had fallen by 0.3% this quarter and lost 11.2% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 2.6% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, one analyst negatively revised an earnings estimate

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is no "strong buy" or "buy," 7 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the property & casualty insurance peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Markel Group Inc is $2,100.00, about 16.2% above its last closing price of $1,759.21

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data April 30 at 12:13 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Samsung Elec Q1 profit surges eightfold to a record
data.description:
Samsung Electronics said on Thursday its operating profit jumped eightfold to a record in the first quarter, underpinned by higher chip prices as an artificial intelligence boom led to a supply crunch.

Samsung expects earnings to improve further in the second quarter, saying investment in AI infrastructure would continue to expand, driving up memory chip prices.

The world's top memory chipmaker posted 57.2 trillion won ($38.43 billion) in operating profit for the January-to-March period, in line with its estimate of 57.2 trillion won and up from 6.69 trillion won a year earlier.

The South Korean company's revenue rose 69% to 133.9 trillion won in the quarter from a year earlier.

The AI data centre boom has spurred chipmakers to shift capacity to making advanced chips used in Nvidia's AI accelerators, squeezing supply of traditional chips and driving up prices.

Samsung Electronics shares rose 1.3% after the earnings announcement.

($1 = 1,488.2900 won)

data.title: New Zealand central bank to make monetary policy committee votes public for greater transparency
data.description:
New Zealand said on Thursday individual votes cast by members of the central bank's monetary policy committee will be made public when consensus is not reached, aiming to enhance transparency and improve better public understanding of its decisions.

Finance Minister Nicola Willis said the changes will ease existing restrictions, allowing committee members more freedom to discuss monetary policy publicly while still adhering to rules that ensure equal access to information.

"The (monetary policy committee) will also begin attributing material differences in views in its records of meetings," Willis said in a statement.

The policy change, effective from Thursday, was agreed by the finance minister and the Reserve Bank of New Zealand's monetary policy committee (MPC), which sets the official cash rate.

Governor Anna Breman said the changes strengthen the transparency of members' views, supporting the committee's accountability.

"These changes ... brings us in line with some of the most transparent central banks in the world," she said.

Breman, a Swedish national who became the RBNZ's first female governor in September, has championed greater openness in monetary policy decisions.

Under her leadership, the RBNZ introduced press conferences after each monetary policy review and increased the annual number of policy meetings to eight from seven, addressing criticisms of insufficient communication during longer breaks.

In the most recent monetary policy meeting this month, discussions included potential advantages of an earlier rate hike, but individual opinions were not attributed though the decision was unanimous.

By contrast, the committee recorded a 5-1 split in voting during its November meeting but did not disclose individual votes.

data.title: Key facts: XRP dips to $1.37; $1.49B open interest; Bitwise $2.80–$4.94
data.description:
• XRPUSD derivatives: open interest ~$1.49B, long/short ~2.4:1. 24h liquidations ~$3.8M (longs ~95.7%). Net whale vs retail delta -0.84, indicating retail net buying.1
  • Bitwise models XRP targets: base about $2.80 and end‑2026 upside near $4.94; a $100 XRP is deemed theoretically possible over decades but currently unlikely.2
  • XRP dipped to $1.3680 then bounced to the 23.6% Fib. Immediate resistance sits at $1.4075–$1.4170; a break above $1.4250 could target $1.4500.3

data.title: Key facts: Kelp Collateral Bad Debt; $307M Raised; Protocol Live
data.description:
• Stolen Kelp DAO tokens were used as collateral on Aave V3, creating fake positions that generated bad debt, amid large withdrawals and sharp drops in borrowing of USDT, USDC and WETH1
  • After a Lazarus Group-linked attack, industry backers pledged funds to support Aave. About $307 million was raised to aid recovery and cover losses within the Aave ecosystem.2
  • AAVEUSD: Aave maintained protocol operations and real-time liquidity adjustments during the disruption; despite outflows, yields and deposits began normalizing as core DeFi functions remained intact.3
  • Aave (AAVEUSD): DeFi groups report Aave V4 security initiatives and funding boosts for Ethereum ecosystem resilience, emphasizing strengthened defenses for Aave-related protocols and governance.4

data.title: Key facts: Dogecoin $0.112 surge, short squeeze; ETP listed; bearish
data.description:
• Dogecoin rose above $0.10 to $0.112 intra‑day, gaining ~15% as futures open interest jumped 28% to $1.81B — the highest since October; reports cited a short squeeze.1
  • Dogecoin (DOGEUSD) rallied after 21Shares launched a physically backed DOGE ETP on Germany’s Xetra, boosting regulated custody, liquidity and trading interest on a major European exchange.2
  • DOGEUSD on the 1-hour chart hit a harmonic D zone and showed bearish rejection. Initial downside targets: $0.0970, $0.0959; extended target: $0.0936. Reclaiming D zone would nullify bearish view.3
  • DOGE broke a descending trendline; Supertrend turned bullish and RSI near 70. Immediate resistance $0.110–$0.118, support $0.100 and lower $0.090–$0.095; holding $0.10 is key.4

data.title: Key facts: BTC OI $61B→$49B; $2.5B liq.; WH previews BTC plan; sup $73.5K
data.description:
• Jan 29–Feb 5 BTCUSD drawdown: futures open interest fell from ~$61B to ~$49B; about $2.5B in BTC positions liquidated; total crypto liquidations ~ $4.5B.1
  • White House adviser Patrick Witt said a public announcement about a strategic Bitcoin (BTCUSD) reserve is forthcoming, explicitly referencing Bitcoin.2
  • BTCUSD: strong support near $73,500; broader downside $71,438–$73,408. Breakout target to fuel upside $80,646; next resistance $86,549; longer-term target $100,739.3
  • U.S. Clarity Act could formalize Bitcoin (BTCUSD) under CFTC jurisdiction, a near-term factor traders watch for potential impact on market structure and stability ahead of Fed moves.4
  • Q2 2026 on-chain data shows BTCUSD’s Net Unrealized Profit/Loss moved into the Optimism band; surveys found ~75% of institutions and ~71% of others viewed Bitcoin as undervalued.5
  • Bitcoin BTCUSD: one-week to one-month realized cap UTXO age dropped to 3.91%, matching Oct 2023 levels near $27,000; similar readings historically appeared before cycle lows within 3–6 months.6
  • BTCUSD exchange whale ratio hit 0.707, highest in a week: top 10 inflows made over 70% of total BTC moving to exchanges near resistance levels.7
  • Blockchain.com unveils Blockchain Wealth for high-net-worth on‑chain BTC investors, offering 24/7 OTC support, access in 70+ jurisdictions, and institutional-grade liquidity for Bitcoin capital.8
  • Trader DonAlt called BTCUSD "kind of alright": strong bounce at support, weak nearby resistance, price unlikely to surge to $80,000 soon; market likely driven by time near term.9
